name: Beef or Venison Jerky
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut meat into thin slices. Meat is easier to cut when partially frozen, allowing it to slice evenly.
2. Combine salt, black pepper, white pepper, red pepper, meat tenderizer, seasoned salt, Accent, garlic powder, Kitchen bouquet, Morton Tender Quick, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, barbecue sauce, and liquid smoke in a bowl.
3. Place the marinade mixture into a sealable plastic bag and add the sliced meat. Seal the bag and refrigerate for 24 hours to allow the meat to marinate.
4. Line the bottom of the oven with foil or place the meat on a rack set in a shallow pan.
5. Dry the meat in the oven on the lowest setting for 6-8 hours, flipping the slices occasionally for even drying.
6. If additional drying is needed, continue in a warm oven until the jerky reaches the desired level of dryness and texture. Ovens with pilot lights are particularly effective for this process.
